summary: Added a C implementation of HSD_AObjAlloc behind NON_MATCHING while keeping the existing inline assembly fallback. The PR also renamed the BSS allocation-data symbol lbl_804C0880 to aobj_alloc_data and propagated that name through aobj.c and variables.h. The author noted that the function appears to match logically, but extern-related instruction usage still prevented it from being enabled as matching.
what_changed: Renamed the BSS symbol lbl_804C0880 to aobj_alloc_data in asm/bss.s.
Updated include/variables.h to refer to aobj_alloc_data instead of lbl_804C0880.
Changed aobj.c extern declaration from HSD_ObjAllocData lbl_804C0880 to HSD_ObjAllocData aobj_alloc_data.
Updated HSD_AObjInitAllocData, HSD_AObjGetAllocData, and HSD_AObjFree to use aobj_alloc_data.
Added a NON_MATCHING C version of HSD_AObjAlloc, with the original inline assembly retained in the #else branch.
Documented a decomp.me scratch link for the HSD_AObjAlloc attempt.
Added a clarifying comment identifying lbl_804DE43C as 1.0F, alongside the existing 0.0F float constant comment.
smart_moves: Preserved build safety by gating the new C implementation under #ifdef NON_MATCHING while retaining the known assembly implementation.
Promoted an anonymous BSS label to a meaningful allocator-data name, making related HSD_AObj allocation functions easier to reason about.
Kept the decomp.me scratch URL in a code comment so future contributors can reproduce or inspect the matching attempt.
Encoded the suspected correct behavior of HSD_AObjAlloc in C: allocate from aobj_alloc_data, assert on NULL, memset the object, set AOBJ_NO_ANIM, initialize framerate to 1.0f, and return the object.
decomp_lessons: Even when a C implementation appears semantically correct, extern symbol handling can affect generated instruction choices enough to require NON_MATCHING.
Renaming allocator globals can clarify surrounding code and make later matching work easier, especially when multiple functions share the same HSD_ObjAllocData.
For allocator functions, compare the C behavior against the assembly sequence in small steps: allocation call, NULL assert path, zero initialization, default field writes, and return.
Leaving assembly in the #else branch is a useful pattern for preserving exact matching while documenting the current best C reconstruction.

naming_conventions.reason: The symbol is used as the HSD_ObjAllocData backing allocation storage for HSD_AObj.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use #ifdef NON_MATCHING to stage a plausible C decompilation without replacing the matching assembly path.
Keep the original asm implementation in the #else branch until instruction generation issues are resolved.
Record external scratch links such as decomp.me attempts directly near the NON_MATCHING implementation.
The stated blocker was not the high-level logic but instruction usage caused by externs, so future work should revisit symbol declarations/storage class and compiler codegen around aobj_alloc_data.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove NON_MATCHING from HSD_AObjAlloc.
Search for further renames of HSD_ObjAllocData BSS labels in baselib.
Look for fixes involving extern declarations affecting instruction usage in matching attempts.
Search for later changes to aobj_alloc_data or HSD_AObj allocation/free behavior.
